title: Accusative Case — The Object of the Sentence
description: Learn how nouns change when they become the direct object — the accusative case

data.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in -a change to -u in the accusative case: káva → kávu.

exercises.prompt: Review the declension pattern for a feminine noun

data.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in -a change the ending to -u in the accusative singular. Žena (nominative) becomes ženu (accusative) when it is the direct object: Vidím ženu. (I see a woman.)

exercises.prompt: Translate the sentence using the accusative case

data.grammar_note: Kniha (feminine, -a) becomes knihu in the accusative. Mám knihu = I have a book.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the accusative form of the noun in parentheses

data.explanation: Voda (feminine, -a) → vodu in accusative. Piju vodu = I drink water.

exercises.prompt: Review the declension pattern for a masculine animate noun

data.explanation: Masculine animate nouns ending in a hard consonant take -a in the accusative singular. Pes (nominative) becomes psa (accusative): Vidím psa. (I see a dog.) Note: the stem vowel -e- drops: pes → ps-a.

exercises.prompt: Choose the correct accusative form of the masculine animate noun

data.explanation: Muž is a masculine animate noun ending in a soft consonant (ž). It takes -e in the accusative: muž → muže.

exercises.prompt: Review the declension pattern for a masculine inanimate noun

data.explanation: Masculine inanimate nouns do NOT change in the accusative singular. Hrad is the same in both nominative and accusative: Vidím hrad. (I see a castle.)

exercises.prompt: Fill in the accusative form — does the masculine inanimate noun change?

data.explanation: Dům is masculine inanimate. Masculine inanimate nouns do not change in the accusative: dům → dům. Vidím dům = I see a house.

exercises.prompt: Review the declension pattern for a neuter noun

data.explanation: Neuter nouns do NOT change in the accusative singular. Město is the same in both nominative and accusative: Vidím město. (I see a city.)

exercises.prompt: Translate — remember neuter nouns don't change in accusative

data.grammar_note: Pivo is neuter. Neuter nouns are unchanged in the accusative: pivo → pivo. Vidím pivo = I see a beer.

exercises.prompt: Listen and write what you hear

data.expected_text: Vidím kávu. Mám knihu. Piju vodu.
data.note: Three short sentences using the feminine accusative -u ending: kávu, knihu, vodu.

exercises.answer_key: Vidím kávu. Mám knihu. Piju vodu.

data.target_text: Vidím ženu. Mám knihu. Piju vodu.
data.translation_en: I see a woman. I have a book. I drink water.

exercises.answer_key: Vidím ženu. Mám knihu. Piju vodu.
